channel.title: Hermes Atlas — new projects
channel.description: Newly added community-built tools, skills, plugins, and integrations for Nous Research's Hermes Agent. Updated daily.

item.title: mnemosyne (Memory & Context)
item.description: Mnemosyne is a local-first memory system designed to provide persistent storage for Hermes Agents without relying on cloud services or external databases. It utilizes a SQLite backend with native vector search and full-text search to manage conversations, preferences, and knowledge in-process. The system implements a three-tier BEAM architecture that handles working memory, episodic memory, and temporary scratchpads while supporting automatic consolidation through sleep cycles. By operating enti

item.title: keep (Memory & Context)
item.description: Keep is a reflective memory system for AI agents that enables long-term learning and contextual awareness. It processes diverse inputs—including URLs, codebases, and media files—by summarizing, embedding, and organizing them into a navigable graph of "edge tags." The system automatically surfaces relevant past learnings and commitments during agent sessions to improve decision-making. It supports fully local operation via Ollama or MLX, as well as cloud-hosted and API-based configurations.

item.title: Web3Hermes (Workspaces & GUIs)
item.description: Web3Hermes is a lightweight WebUI for Hermes Agent specifically localized for Chinese-speaking users. It provides a desktop-optimized interface that simplifies interaction with the agent through a localized workflow and full Chinese language support. The platform integrates a built-in workspace file browser, session management tools, and support for multiple AI models including OpenAI, Anthropic, and DeepSeek. Users can manage tasks, edit code with syntax highlighting, and visualize tool calls w

item.title: hermes-web-ui (Workspaces & GUIs)
item.description: Hermes Web UI is a comprehensive dashboard for managing Hermes Agent, providing a centralized interface for AI interactions and system administration. It simplifies agent operations by integrating multi-session chat, usage analytics, and automated cron job scheduling into a single responsive web environment. Users can configure eight different communication platforms, manage model providers, and browse files across local or remote backends. The system also features multi-profile management, grou

item.title: hermes-otel (Plugins & Extensions)
item.description: hermes-otel is an observability plugin for the Hermes Agent that enables comprehensive monitoring of agentic workflows. It automatically captures and exports LLM tool calls, model invocations, and API requests as OpenTelemetry spans to any OTLP-compatible backend. The plugin provides deep visibility into session lifecycles, including parent-child span nesting and token usage aggregation. It supports a wide range of observability platforms including Phoenix, Langfuse, SigNoz, and Grafana for trac

item.title: hermes-telegram-miniapp (Workspaces & GUIs)
item.description: Hermes Telegram Mini App is a React-based terminal interface that allows users to manage and interact with Hermes agents directly within the Telegram ecosystem. It establishes a secure mobile-first workspace by wrapping the Hermes CLI in a dark-mode web UI, featuring streaming chat and system monitoring. The application utilizes dual HMAC-SHA256 and Ed25519 validation to ensure secure access via Telegram's authentication layer. Users can spawn background agent instances, manage scheduled cron jo

item.title: hermes-relay (Integrations & Bridges)
item.description: Hermes-Relay is a companion suite that enables remote control and interaction with a server-deployed Hermes agent via Android and desktop interfaces. It uses a WebSocket Secure (WSS) relay and a dedicated server plugin to bridge the gap between a remote agent's brain and a user's local hardware. The Android app allows the agent to read screens and perform device actions like swiping or typing, while the desktop CLI enables the remote agent to execute local terminal commands and access files. Thi

item.title: hermes-lcm (Memory & Context)
item.description: Hermes-LCM is a Lossless Context Management plugin for Hermes Agent that prevents information loss during long-running conversations. It replaces standard lossy compression with a hierarchical Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) that persists every message in an immutable-first SQLite store. When context limits are reached, the engine uses three levels of escalation—from detailed summaries to deterministic truncation—while providing the agent with specialized tools to retrieve original details. This ar

item.title: hermesclaw (Integrations & Bridges)
item.description: HermesClaw is an integration bridge that enables a single WeChat account to run both Hermes Agent and OpenClaw simultaneously. It functions as a central iLink poller that creates two local proxy servers, allowing each agent gateway to receive messages without token conflicts or 403 errors. Users can toggle between agents or trigger simultaneous responses using commands like /hermes, /openclaw, or /both. The tool maintains native support for voice transcriptions and media files by forwarding raw

item.title: hermes-claude-code-rc (Integrations & Bridges)
item.description: This Hermes Agent plugin enables remote management of Claude Code sessions through a Telegram interface. It bridges the gap between mobile devices and local filesystems by allowing users to initiate authenticated remote control sessions on their host machine via slash commands. The plugin captures the resulting session URL and returns it to the user, enabling full access to local tools and databases from the Claude mobile app. Beyond session management, it supports headless command execution and

item.title: hermes-ui (Workspaces & GUIs)
item.description: Hermes UI is a glassmorphic web interface for the self-hosted Hermes Agent, providing a visual workspace for AI interaction and system management. It operates as a single-file React application served via a lightweight Python proxy, requiring no complex build steps or external dependencies. The interface surfaces advanced agent features such as real-time log streaming, MCP tool browsing, and a dedicated artifact panel for rendering HTML, SVG, and PDF outputs. Users can manage model providers, mo

item.title: gbrain (Memory & Context)
item.description: GBrain is an opinionated memory and context layer designed to give AI agents a persistent, self-wiring knowledge base. It utilizes a hybrid search system and a self-constructing knowledge graph to extract entities and create typed links between people, companies, and events without requiring LLM calls for graph construction. The system consolidates memory through overnight autonomous jobs that fix citations and enrich data from meetings, emails, and media. It integrates with Hermes Agent and Ope

item.title: hermes-webui (Workspaces & GUIs)
item.description: Hermes WebUI provides a lightweight, browser-based interface for the Hermes Agent, offering a visual alternative to the standard command-line environment. Built with Python and vanilla JavaScript, the application maintains full parity with the CLI without requiring complex build steps or frameworks. The interface features a three-panel layout for managing chat sessions, workspace files, and agent controls like model selection and token usage monitoring. It allows users to interact with their age

item.title: clawshell (Plugins & Extensions)
item.description: ClawShell is a security-privileged process that acts as a safety harness for the Hermes Agent ecosystem by protecting sensitive credentials and PII. It functions as a proxy between the agent and upstream LLM providers, swapping virtual keys for real ones stored in a restricted directory to ensure the agent never has direct access to credentials. The system performs Data Loss Prevention (DLP) scanning to redact or block sensitive information in request and response bodies. Additionally, it provid

item.title: hermes-desktop (Workspaces & GUIs)
item.description: Hermes Desktop is a native macOS application that provides a localized workspace for managing Hermes Agent hosts over SSH. It connects directly to remote environments to manage sessions, skills, and cron jobs without requiring a gateway API, local file mirroring, or remote daemon. The app features a multi-tabbed terminal and profile-aware views that surface usage trends and canonical file states directly from the host. This allows users to maintain a persistent, native interface for multi-agent

item.title: hermes-ecosystem (Guides & Docs)
item.description: Hermes Atlas is a community-curated directory and ecosystem map for the Hermes Agent by Nous Research. It aggregates over 80 quality-filtered repositories, including skills, plugins, and deployment templates, using a vanilla JavaScript frontend and Vercel serverless functions. The platform features a RAG-powered chatbot that answers technical questions grounded in 27 research files using hybrid retrieval and OpenRouter LLMs. Users can search and filter the ecosystem while viewing live GitHub sta

item.title: hermes-control-interface (Workspaces & GUIs)
item.description: The Hermes Control Interface is a self-hosted web dashboard designed to manage the Hermes AI agent stack through a centralized graphical interface. It utilizes a Node.js and Express backend with WebSockets to provide real-time terminal access, file management, and multi-agent gateway control. Users can monitor system metrics, track token usage analytics, and configure agent skills or cron jobs without using the command line. The platform includes a revamped chat interface with tool call visualiz

item.title: hermes-skill-distillation (Forks & Derivatives)
item.description: This project provides a skill distillation pipeline designed to fine-tune Hermes 4 models using real-world agentic trajectories. It captures tool-use sessions from the Hermes agent and converts them into high-quality training data through a specialized environment and automated scoring system. The pipeline supports both offline SFT data generation and live reinforcement learning via integration with the Atropos training framework. By focusing on grounded tasks like coding and system administrati

item.title: hermes-alpha (Forks & Derivatives)
item.description: Hermes Alpha is an experimental framework designed to test if a stock AI agent can autonomously bootstrap and manage a bug bounty system from first principles. The architecture utilizes a two-agent loop where a persistent 'Overseer' agent builds, deploys, and refines an ephemeral 'Hunter' agent tasked with finding software vulnerabilities. By providing the agent with only a Linux terminal and an identity document rather than custom tools, the project evaluates the self-improvement capabilities o

item.title: hermes-agent-camel (Forks & Derivatives)
item.description: This project is a specialized fork of Hermes Agent that integrates CaMeL trust-boundary principles to defend against prompt-injection attacks during autonomous execution. It implements a security layer that monitors or enforces policies to block unauthorized sensitive actions when the agent is operating under untrusted contexts. Users can explicitly opt into these protections using specific CLI flags while maintaining access to the core Hermes features like autonomous skill creation and multi-pl

item.title: HermesWiki (Guides & Docs)
item.description: HermesWiki is a centralized documentation repository designed to support users of the Hermes autonomous AI email agent. It provides a structured knowledge base that outlines deployment patterns and practical implementation recipes for the agent. By consolidating technical guidance, the project helps developers configure and optimize their AI-driven email workflows. These resources ensure that users can effectively navigate the setup and operational requirements of the Hermes ecosystem.

item.title: hermes-agent-docs (Guides & Docs)
item.description: Hermes Agent is an autonomous AI system designed for persistent learning and multi-platform deployment across various messaging and development environments. It utilizes a closed learning loop to create reusable skills from experience, manage long-term memory via SQLite, and maintain a consistent user model across sessions. The agent supports concurrent tool execution and integrates with dozens of model providers and messaging gateways, including Telegram, Discord, and Slack. Its architecture al

item.title: hermes-wsl-ubuntu (Guides & Docs)
item.description: This project provides a comprehensive setup guide and automation script for running Hermes Agent locally on Windows via WSL2, Linux, or macOS. It integrates llama.cpp for model inference with Qwen3.5 GGUF models, utilizing CUDA or Metal acceleration for optimized performance. The guide covers the full stack including the Camofox browser server for web-browsing tasks and the Hermes HUDUI for a graphical user interface. Users can deploy a production-ready agentic environment with local LLM support

item.title: hermes-optimization-guide (Guides & Docs)
item.description: The Hermes Optimization Guide is a comprehensive technical resource and toolkit designed to move Hermes Agent deployments from basic installation to production-ready environments. It provides a 21-part curriculum alongside runnable artifacts, including a one-command VPS bootstrap script, hardened systemd units, and pre-configured Caddy templates for secure reverse proxying. Users can implement opinionated configurations for cost-optimization, security hardening, and multi-platform integration ac

item.title: hermes-agent-orange-book (Guides & Docs)
item.description: The Hermes Agent Orange Book is a practical guide designed to help users master the Hermes Agent open-source AI framework. It explains the framework's technical architecture, including its three-layer memory system, self-improving learning loop, and automatic skill evolution. The guide provides 17 chapters of content covering installation, customization, and real-world applications such as development automation and knowledge assistants.

item.title: awesome-hermes-agent (Guides & Docs)
item.description: This repository is a curated collection of resources, tools, and community-developed skills designed for the Hermes Agent ecosystem. It organizes the landscape of the self-improving AI agent by categorizing official Nous Research projects, third-party skill libraries, and deployment utilities. Users can find specialized integrations for messaging platforms like Telegram and Discord, as well as meta-skills that allow the agent to auto-generate its own capabilities from workflows. The list serves

item.title: hermes-embodied (Domain Applications)
item.description: Hermes Embodied is a robotics extension for Hermes Agent that enables autonomous fine-tuning of Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models through natural language. The system orchestrates a self-improvement loop by collecting robot trajectories in simulation or hardware, provisioning cloud GPUs via Vast.ai, and retraining models when performance thresholds are met. It integrates with the LeRobot framework and MuJoCo simulation to automate the transition from data collection to model promotion. This al

item.title: mercury (Domain Applications)
item.description: Mercury is a blockchain cash flow analyzer and Hermes Agent skill designed to provide transparency into wallet activities across Ethereum, Base, and Solana. It utilizes parallel subagents to fetch transaction data, identify six specific fraud patterns, and generate AI-driven trading strategies based on wallet behavior. The project features a multi-layer WebGL and Canvas dashboard for real-time visualization of inflows, outflows, and risk metrics. It operates as a standalone tool or an integrated

item.title: hermescraft (Domain Applications)
item.description: HermesCraft is an embodied AI framework that integrates Hermes agents into Minecraft as persistent, interactive players. It connects the Hermes agent stack to a Mineflayer bot body through a custom Minecraft CLI and HTTP API, allowing agents to perceive and interact with the game world. Agents can function as individual companions or as part of multi-agent civilizations with private messaging and social memory. The system emphasizes fair-play perception by restricting agent knowledge to line-of-

item.title: infra-monitoring-toolkit (Domain Applications)
item.description: This project is a reference implementation for building autonomous infrastructure monitoring systems using Hermes Agent. It utilizes cron-based workflows to ingest AI research from arXiv and track trending infrastructure repositories on GitHub. The system automatically generates structured reports on compute risk levels, infrastructure trends, and cost projections. It serves as a template for developers to deploy long-running, headless agentic systems for operational intelligence.
